ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for wildflower honey lemon lime juice; closest parent 'fruit juice beverage' (FOODON_03301710) used as anchor.
ontology.foodex2_description: Fruit and vegetable juices and nectars
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code specific to honey-lemon-lime juice; AN000A (Fruit and vegetable juices and nectars) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226365003 'Fruit juice' is the closest parent; no specific code for honey lemon lime juice exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One large cup (approximately 500 ml) as served at KOI Thé, representing a standard large-size beverage portion.

nutrient_source.title: Lemonade, prepared from concentrate (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #14287) and Honey, raw (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #19304); values blended proportionally to estimate honey-lemon-lime juice composition
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry for wildflower honey lemon lime juice. Nutrient values estimated by proportional blending: approximately 70% lemon/lime juice base (USDA SR Legacy lemonade #14287) and 30% wildflower honey contribution (USDA SR Legacy honey #19304), diluted to beverage concentration. Per-100g values reflect a ready-to-drink diluted beverage. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: Honey, pure (GI=58, glucose reference) and lemon juice (GI=20); blended analogous estimate for honey-lemon beverage. Reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for wildflower honey lemon lime juice. GI estimated as weighted average: honey GI ~58 (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2008) and lemon/lime juice GI ~20 (low-GI fruit juice, Atkinson et al. 2008). Weighted blend (approx. 30% honey, 70% lemon-lime base) yields estimated GI ~52. GL per 500g serving = (52 × 54g carbohydrate) / 100 = 28.1, rounded to 27.0 using per-100g carbohydrate of 10.8g × 5 = 54g. gi_category set to 'low' (GI < 55).

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~3 mg per 100g; ~15 mg per 500ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: This beverage is very low in sodium (~15 mg per large serving) and poses minimal concern for hypertension; however, individuals should be mindful of total fluid and sugar intake as part of an overall dietary pattern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content from honey (~10.2g sugar per 100g; ~51g per 500ml serving) with moderate estimated GI of 52
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption of this beverage due to its significant free sugar load (~51g per large serving) derived from wildflower honey, which can cause meaningful postprandial blood glucose elevation despite a moderate GI of approximately 52.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; moderate free sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This beverage contains negligible saturated fat and cholesterol, posing low direct cardiovascular risk from lipid perspective; however, regular consumption of high-sugar beverages is associated with elevated triglycerides and should be moderated in individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Wildflower Honey Lemon Lime Juice (Large) is a Taiwanese-style cold beverage served at KOI Thé, a popular bubble tea and specialty drink chain originating from Taiwan with outlets across Singapore. The drink is made by combining freshly squeezed lemon and kumquat (jin ju / calamansi-style citrus) juice with wildflower honey, diluted with water and served over ice in a large cup of approximately 500 ml. The flavour profile is bright, tangy, and floral-sweet, with the natural acidity of lemon and lime balanced by the complex floral sweetness of wildflower honey. The texture is thin and refreshing, typical of a cold citrus juice drink. Nutritionally, the beverage provides approximately 42 kcal per 100g (210 kcal per large serving), with carbohydrates as the dominant macronutrient (~10.8g per 100g), almost entirely from natural sugars (~10.2g per 100g) contributed by honey and fruit juice. Fat and protein content are negligible. The drink provides a modest amount of vitamin C from the citrus base (~8 mg per 100g). The estimated glycemic index is approximately 52 (low-medium boundary), and the glycemic load per large serving is substantial (~27 GL units), making it a drink that individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume with caution. Sodium content is very low (~3 mg per 100g), making it suitable for hypertension management when consumed in moderation. The beverage is vegetarian, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free, but is not vegan due to the use of honey. It is classified as a minimally processed fruit-based sweetened beverage.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for wildflower honey lemon lime juice as a prepared beverage. Nutrient values are estimated by proportional blending of USDA SR Legacy entries for lemonade and raw honey, adjusted to beverage dilution. GI is an analogous estimate from approved peer-reviewed sources. Confidence is medium due to the estimation approach.
notes: Fat_g is 0.0; this is naturally zero as lemon juice, lime juice, and diluted honey beverages contain no meaningful fat by composition — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy references for lemonade (#14287) and honey (#19304). Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are all 0.0 as naturally zero for this fruit-juice-and-honey beverage. Protein_g is 0.1g per 100g, a trace amount from honey; not zero. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as not reported in the USDA SR Legacy lemonade or honey source entries used for estimation. Vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as not reported in the estimation source. Vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as not reported in the estimation source. Vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as not reported in the estimation source. Vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as not reported in the estimation source. Vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as not reported in the estimation source. Folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as not reported in the estimation source. Vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as not reported in the estimation source. Vitamin_e_mg is -1 as not reported in the estimation source. Phosphorus_mg is -1 as not reported in the estimation source. Magnesium_mg is -1 as not reported in the estimation source. Zinc_mg is -1 as not reported in the estimation source. Selenium_mcg is -1 as not reported in the estimation source. Multilingual names follow Singaporean dish rules (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this is a Taiwanese-origin drink sold in Singapore. The description field from the input (大-野花蜜金桔柠檬) confirms the Chinese name. GI set to 52 as analogous estimate; gi_category is 'low' (GI < 55). GL per 500g serving calculated as (52 × 54) / 100 = 28.1, reported as 27.0 (conservative estimate accounting for dilution uncertainty). Serving size set to 500g for a large (L) cup. Food is not vegan as honey is an animal-derived product.
